basic_info.title: Chrono Trigger
basic_info.review: Chrono Trigger is a masterpiece of the RPG genre, offering a perfect blend of engaging story, innovative combat, and emotional depth that set a new standard for the medium.

similar_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time

play_today.details: Widely considered the definitive version by the community. It includes the original SNES content plus the Dream Devourer dungeon, new characters (Lynx), and voice acting for cutscenes. Features a dual-screen interface for maps and inventory, plus a modernized translation that fixes many SNES-era errors. While the soundtrack is rearranged, the added content and QoL features make it the most complete experience.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger DS High Resolution Texture Pack
recommended_patches.description: Replaces the low-resolution DS sprites with upscaled, high-definition textures to improve visual clarity on modern screens while retaining the original DS content.

optimal_settings: Enable 4x internal resolution for sharp visuals
Use OpenGL renderer for better performance
Enable audio interpolation for smoother sound
Set frame skip to 0 for smooth gameplay
Enable touch controls overlay for mobile play

play_today.details: The original 1995 release offers the purest artistic vision with the original soundtrack and no voice acting, which many purists prefer. It requires an emulator or original hardware. The English version is available but lacks the quality of life improvements found in later ports.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger Bugfix and Uncensoring Patch
recommended_patches.description: A comprehensive patch that fixes various glitches present in the original SNES release, such as the 'Lucca's Gun' bug and the 'Save Point' glitch. It also restores censored content, including the original 'Lizard Man' sprite and dialogue that was altered in the North American release.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger English Translation Patch (Fan Translation)
recommended_patches.description: An unofficial translation patch that improves upon the original English localization by correcting mistranslations, adding missing context, and restoring Japanese cultural nuances that were lost in the official Square release.

play_today.details: A port of the SNES version that includes a new orchestral soundtrack arrangement and a new dungeon. However, it suffers from slower load times and lacks the touch-screen convenience of the DS version. It is generally considered inferior to the DS port for modern play.

The most convenient modern option available on Steam. It is based on the DS version, meaning it includes the Lost Sanctum, voice acting, and animated cutscenes. It features native widescreen support, controller remapping, and cloud saves. While the graphics are upscaled, the lack of a native 4K texture pack and occasional UI scaling issues prevent it from being perfect.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger PC Widescreen Fix
recommended_patches.description: Fixes UI scaling issues on ultrawide monitors and ensures the game renders correctly in native resolutions beyond 1080p.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger PC High Resolution Texture Pack
recommended_patches.description: Replaces the default upscaled DS textures with community-created high-resolution assets for sharper visuals on 4K displays.

play_today.details: The original experience with the authentic soundtrack composed by Yasunori Mitsuda and the classic pixel art. Preferred by purists who want the game exactly as it was released in 1995. Lacks the extra content of the DS version but offers the most faithful representation of the original design. Requires emulation for modern play, but offers the best audio fidelity for the original score.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger Bugfix and Uncensoring Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes critical bugs like the 'Lucca's Gun' glitch and restores censored content including the original Lizard Man sprite and dialogue.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger+
recommended_patches.description: Adds a ton of cut content back into the game, allows access to previously unexplorable areas, and includes additional music tracks.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger: Bugfix and Uncensoring Patch
recommended_patches.description: Fixes critical bugs like the 'Lucca's Gun' glitch and restores censored content including the original Lizard Man sprite and dialogue.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger: Complete
recommended_patches.description: Updates the script to modern standards while preserving the original translation's character, combining the best of the SNES and DS scripts.

recommended_patches.name: Chrono Trigger: ATB Fixes
recommended_patches.description: Resolves subtle bugs in the Active Time Battle system where multiple actions resolve incorrectly or inputs are lost during animations.

optimal_settings: Enable SuperFX emulation for accurate hardware behavior
Set audio to 44100Hz for original fidelity
Use CRT shader for authentic retro feel
Enable accurate timing for precise gameplay
Use high-quality audio resampling for clear sound
Set internal resolution to 2x for clean scaling

play_today.details: A convenient mobile port based on the DS version, offering the same extra content and voice acting. Ideal for playing on the go with controller support (MFi or Bluetooth). The interface is optimized for touch, though some users find the on-screen controls less precise than a physical controller. Includes cloud save support via Game Center, making it a solid alternative to emulation.

description.synopsis: In the year 1000 AD, a young man named Crono is interrupted during a festival by a mysterious woman named Marle, who is chased by a robot. After a strange incident involving a time portal, Crono and Marle are transported to the year 65,000,000 BC, where they meet a dinosaur named Lucca. Together, they must navigate through different eras to prevent a catastrophic future and uncover the truth behind the mysterious robot.
key_features: Active Time Battle (ATB) system with real-time movement
Party-based combat with up to seven selectable characters
Non-linear time travel allowing access to seven distinct eras
Multiple endings determined by player choices and sequence of events
Dual-weapon system allowing characters to equip two weapons simultaneously
Techno system combining two characters' abilities for powerful attacks
No traditional random encounters; enemies visible on overworld map
Dynamic soundtrack that changes based on era and context
Fast-forwarding time to skip battles or travel between eras
New Game+ mode allowing retention of stats and items
legacy_and_impact: Chrono Trigger is widely regarded as one of the greatest video games of all time, influencing countless RPGs that followed with its time-travel mechanics and narrative structure. Its critical acclaim has remained consistent across decades, with modern ports introducing it to new generations of players.
